rulings.Case Title: MARCELLA FLUNO VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Order Staying Action Between The Arbitrating Parties Only And Requiring Pltf To Arbitrate The Controversy
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 14, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT BRIAN ANDREWS Petition for Order Staying Action Between the Arbitrating Parties Only and Requiring Plaintiff to Arbitrate the Controversy. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. DAVID BASS et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 14, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS DAVID BASS, and, MIHUI BASS Motion for Order Vacating and Setting Aside Default and Default Judgment. Is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK LIPIAN MD et al VS. BRIAN T. ANDREWS MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Pettion For Order Staying Action Between The Arbitrating Parties Only And Requiring Pltf To Arbitrate The Controversy
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 14, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT BRIAN ANDREWS Petition for Order Staying Action Between the Arbitrating Parties Only and Requiring Plaintiff to Arbitrate the Controversy. Is granted. Both plaintiffs will arbitrate their claims. See Gross vs. Recabaren (1988) 206 Cal.App. 3rd 771 and Mormile vs. Sinclair (1994) 21 Cal.App. 4th 1508. This action is stayed pending complete of the arbitration. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK LIPIAN MD et al VS. BRIAN T. ANDREWS MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 14, 2010, line 6, PLAINTIFFS MARK LIPIAN, and, MARY LIPIAN Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint. Off Calendar, as moot, in light of the order granting stay. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BAY CITIES PAVING & GRADING, INC. VS. DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES, LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Confirm Arbitratrion Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 14, 2010, line 12, PETITIONER BAY CITIES PAVING & GRADING, INC. Motion to Confirm Arbitration Award. Is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BAY CITIES PAVING & GRADING, INC. VS. DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES, LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 14, 2010, line 12, RESPONDENT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES Motion to Vacate Arbitration Award. Is granted. Relief granted under the court's equitable powers. Respondent's mistake was extrinsic and it moved diligently to seek relief upon its personal receipt of the award. (See Demello vs. Souza (1973) 36 Cal.App. 3rd 79, 84-86. =(302/CWW)
